executive_summary.question: Under what circumstances does the right of private defence of the body NOT extend to voluntarily causing the assailant's death?
executive_summary.generated_answer: The right of private defence of the body does not extend to the voluntary causing of death to the assailant if the offence is not of the descriptions specified in section 38. Specifically, if the offence is not an assault that may reasonably cause the apprehension of death or grievous hurt, the right of private defence of the body does not extend to causing death. [Chunk-ID: d3d93d06-b511-4735-9b84-6085daecad6e]
